element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Primary weapon introduced against Tyrannosaurus boss encounter
Range weapon for varied combat scenarios
Advanced elemental weapon for challenging encounters
Time pressure element affecting all levels except boss battles
Non-linear progression choice system in arcade and most ports

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Continuous pressure via passive health drain punctuated by climactic boss encounters; momentum-based combat flow with weapon variety changes

spatial_grammar.optional_content_signal: Bonus stages accessible from overworld map (SNES version feature); non-linear level selection in some ports

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction via first boss (Tyrannosaurus) with basic stone wheel weapon; establishes core combat and health drain mechanics
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Weapon variety expands; enemy density increases; health pressure becomes more critical; elemental weapons introduce tactical variation
difficulty_curve.late_game: Advanced elemental weapons (electricity); complex boss patterns; health management becomes critical skill; final boss significantly different in SNES version
notable_spikes: Final boss encounter (version-dependent, SNES variant unique)
Boss progression requiring route selection strategy (arcade/Genesis/MS-DOS/Amiga/Zeebo versions)

notable_level_patterns.description: Each level culminates in boss encounter; arcade and most ports allow player to select route after defeat for non-linear progression
SNES version uniquely features overworld map for level selection with integrated bonus stages; deviates from linear arcade design
SNES version implements longer individual levels compared to arcade; increases level complexity and play duration
Arcade and most ports offer three exit choices after final boss, leading to slightly different ending sequences; SNES version reduces to two endings

data_quality_notes: T3 tier data: qualitative information extracted from gameplay descriptions and port comparisons. Specific timing, enemy patterns, and exact level counts not documented in sources. Boss encounters and weapon introduction sequence inferred from gameplay descriptions and Wikipedia overview.
regional_variant_impact: SNES version significantly restructured with overworld map, longer levels, missing weapons, different final boss, and reduced endings (2 vs 3 in arcade/Genesis). This affects element introduction order and spatial grammar compared to arcade original.
